channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: South Korea's spy agency said North Korea has sent troops to support Russia's war in Ukraine, transferring 1,500 North Korean special operation forces to the Russian city of Vladivostok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline amid a massive blackout and after a major power plant fails
item.description: Cuba’s electrical grid has gone offline after one of the island’s major power plants failed and as a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Rodríguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's foreign minister says India's remaining diplomats are on notice not to harm Canadians
item.description: Canada’s foreign minister says India’s remaining diplomats in the country are “clearly on notice” not to endanger Canadian lives.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK Foreign Secretary Lammy urges China against supporting Russia's military
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Britain's foreign secretary raised concerns about China's support of Russia in its war against Ukraine on Friday, urging his Chinese counterpart to prevent Chinese firms from supplying Russia's military in a meeting in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Yahya Sinwar, the Hamas leader who was killed in Gaza
item.description: The Hamas leader who was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza, Yahya Sinwar, was the mastermind behind the Oct. 7 attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexican newspaper offices hit by gunfire in Sinaloa state capital
item.description: Assailants have fired a dozen gunshots at a building housing the newspaper El Debate in the embattled northern state of Sinaloa.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia returns 500 dead soldiers to Ukraine as world leaders mull next steps
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia on Friday returned to Ukraine the bodies of 501 soldiers, Ukrainian authorities said, in what appeared to be the biggest repatriation of war dead since Russia’s full-scale invasion in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Indian government employee charged in US assassination plot
item.description: The Justice Department announced charges against a former Indian government employee in the murder plot against dual U.S.-Canadian citizen Gurpatwant Singh Pannun, a prominent Sikh separatist.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: North Korea sending 10K troops to fight with Russia, Zelensky says
item.description: North Korea is sending around 10,000 troops to Ukraine to fight alongside Russian forces, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's spy agency said Friday that North Korea has dispatched troops to support Russia's war against Ukraine. If confirmed, the move would bring a third country into the war and intensify a standoff between North Korea and the West.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas admits 'painful, distressing' losses after Israeli video shows terrorist Sinwar moments before his death
item.description: Hamas is admitting to suffering "very painful and distressing" losses following the killing of leader Yahya Sinwar as IDF footage reveals his final moments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas signal resistance to ending Gaza war after Sinwar's death
item.description: Hamas has confirmed that its leader, Yahya Sinwar, was killed by Israeli forces in Gaza and reiterated its stance that hostages the militant group took from Israel a year ago will not be released until there is a cease-fire in Gaza and a withdrawal of Israeli troops.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, The Hill

item.title: Rights groups urge Thailand to release Vietnamese activist, saying he faces torture if extradited
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A group of nearly three dozen rights groups called on Thailand's prime minister Friday to release a Vietnamese activist who has been ordered extradited home to face imprisonment on terrorism charges, saying he faces the possibility of torture if returned.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Military voters see 'direct attack' from GOP election challenges
item.description: Republicans are coming under fire from veterans and military groups for attempting to restrict the votes of overseas service members through a series of lawsuits in battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: An American was reportedly kidnapped from a southern Philippine town
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The Philippine police said Friday it has launched a search after gunmen reportedly abducted an American national, who was shot in the leg as he tried to resist before being spirited away from a southern Philippine coastal town by speedboat.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia flaunts its many doomsday weapons to keep the West from ramping up support for Ukraine
item.description: This year has seen President Vladimir Putin repeatedly brandish the nuclear sword, reminding everyone that Russia has the world’s largest atomic arsenal to try to deter the West from ramping up support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentine authorities probe what happened before Liam Payne's fatal fall from his hotel balcony
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — The circumstances surrounding the death of ex-One Direction singer Liam Payne were suspicious and possibly involved drugs, though there was no sign of a third party being involved, Argentine prosecutors said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kim stresses to North Korean troops they should treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un reminded his troops to treat South Korea as a hostile foreign enemy and insisted that the North wouldn’t hesitate to attack its rival if the South infringes upon its sovereignty, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Efforts to combat deforestation in Colombia hindered because of armed groups, report finds
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Armed groups operating in Colombia's Amazon are tightening their grip on the region and that's stalling government efforts to tackle deforestation, according to a think tank report Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sinwar's removal from battlefield is ‘significant,’ presents opportunity for cease-fire, State Department says
item.description: The State Department on Thursday said the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar presents an "opportunity" for a potential cease-fire and an end to the yearlong war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sinwar's killing opens up opportunity and much uncertainty for the war in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s killing of Yahya Sinwar, Hamas’ top leader and the mastermind of the group’s Oct. 7 2023, attack, is a dramatic turning point in the brutal yearlong war that it touched off.
item.dc:creator: Tia Goldenberg And Jack Jeffery, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas leader's killing leaves US, Israel sending different signals
item.description: The death of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar is raising new questions over the course of the war and the fate of hostages still held by the terrorist group, with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Harris: Israeli killing of Sinwar an opportunity to end war in Gaza
item.description: Vice President Harris on Thursday praised Israel's killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, calling for it to be an opportunity for the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza to end after more than a year of fighting in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Hunger experts say the risk of famine in Gaza remains high
item.description: The Gaza Strip is still at risk of famine more than a year into the Israel-Hamas war, even as the number of people facing the most extreme level of hunger has declined in recent months, the international authority on hunger crises said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Roman-era graves found by homeowner during basement renovation project
item.description: During the winter of 2023-2024, archaeologists were called to a home in the suburbs of Paris after a homeowner discovered a skeleton during basement renovations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's former armed forces chief endorses 'victory plan' in first speech since his dismissal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukraine's former commander-in-chief, Valerii Zaluzhnyi, on Thursday expressed his support for President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s "victory plan," despite the tensions between the two that led to his dismissal.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas terror leader Yahya Sinwar killed in Gaza, Israel says
item.description: Israeli officials announced Thursday that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was killed during military operations inside the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea says its revised constitution defines South Korea as 'hostile state' for first time
item.description: South Korea condemned the North's constitutional revision as “an anti-unification, anti-national act.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation
